## Plot distribution of antibodies {.unnumbered}


### Description

<code>autoplot()</code> method for <code>pop_data</code> objects



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>## S3 method for class 'pop_data'
autoplot(object, log = FALSE, type = "density", strata = NULL, ...)
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="object">object</code></td>
<td>
A <code>pop_data</code> object (from <code>load_pop_data()</code>)
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="log">log</code></td>
<td>
whether to show antibody responses on logarithmic scale
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="type">type</code></td>
<td>
an option to choose type of chart:
the current options are <code>"density"</code> or <code>"age-scatter"</code>
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="strata">strata</code></td>
<td>
the name of a variable in <code>pop_data</code>
to stratify by (or <code>NULL</code> for no stratification)
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="...">...</code></td>
<td>
unused
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

a ggplot2::ggplot object



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
library("serocalculator")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(magrittr)

xs_data <-
  serocalculator_example("example_pop_data.csv") |>
  read.csv() |>
  as_pop_data()

xs_data |> autoplot(strata = "catchment", type = "density")
xs_data |> autoplot(strata = "catchment", type = "age-scatter")



```
